title: The Housing Squeeze
subtitle: Everyone needs a roof. Not everyone agrees how to build one.

turns.prompt: Rents hit record highs. Young workers are priced out of every major city. Protesters occupy empty office blocks.

choices.label: Rezone greenbelt land for housing

delayed.headline: New suburbs sprawl faster than services can follow

choices.reaction: Developers celebrate. Environmental groups block roads — then get sued.

choices.label: Cap rents in pressured cities
choices.reaction: Tenants breathe easier. Landlords threaten to sell up.

choices.label: Subsidise first-time buyers

delayed.headline: Subsidies inflate prices — sellers win, buyers still struggle

choices.reaction: Estate agents report a rush. Economists update their bubble charts — again.

turns.prompt: Headlines soften. A few projects break ground. Approval ticks up — for now.

choices.label: Launch a public housing programme
choices.reaction: Waiting lists shorten — slowly. The treasury groans.

choices.label: Fast-track planning for developers
choices.reaction: Cranes multiply. Neighbours sue over every permit.

choices.label: Tax empty and second homes
choices.reaction: Holiday-home owners protest. Some properties hit the market.

turns.prompt: Prices are still rising. Construction costs spike. Something has to give.

choices.label: Ease visas for construction workers
choices.reaction: Sites fill with new crews. Cultural backlash builds in the press.

choices.label: Cut housing benefits to force affordability
choices.reaction: Landlords drop rents — in some areas. Homelessness rises in others.

choices.label: Ban non-resident property purchases
choices.flavour: Homes for people who live here.
choices.reaction: Investors pull capital. Locals cheer — then notice fewer new builds.

turns.prompt: The trade-offs from earlier choices collide. There are no clean fixes left.

memory_callbacks.text: Rent caps you introduced are now shrinking the rental supply you promised to protect.
The sprawl you approved is choking commutes — and your approval with it.
Buyer subsidies you funded are still pumping prices upward.

choices.label: Emergency modular housing blitz
choices.reaction: Families move in. Architects wince. Markets watch the borrowing.

choices.label: Sell public land to fund builds
choices.reaction: Treasury breathes. Future generations inherit less — and know it.

choices.label: Hold the line and weather the storm
choices.reaction: Markets appreciate the discipline. Tent cities grow quietly.

turns.prompt: Final reckoning. What kind of housing market did you leave behind?

choices.label: Prioritise homeowners and builders
choices.reaction: Asset owners celebrate. Renters write angry op-eds.

choices.label: Lock in tenant protections forever
choices.reaction: Tenants feel seen. Investment in new housing dries up — then rents rise anyway.

choices.label: Deregulate and let the market reset
choices.reaction: Prices gyrate. Some win big. Many learn what collateral means.
